Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard P2P flow generally: 1. Create Purchase Requisition 2. Approve Purchase Requisition 3. Issue Purchase Order (PO) - sent to vendor after approval 4. Receive Goods - goods/services arrive from vendor 5. Vendor Invoice Received - vendor sends invoice 6. Match Invoice to PO - verify invoice against PO and goods receipt (three-way match) 7. Release Payment - pay after matching/verification 8. Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Important control points: - No PO should be issued before requisition approval (segregation of duties, authorization) - Payment should not be released before goods receipt and invoice matching (three-way match) - Goods shouldnt be received before PO is issued (theres no authorized order) - Invoice matching should happen before payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1001: 1. Create PR (08:00) 2. Approve PR (08:15) 3. Issue PO (09:00) 4. Receive Goods (next day 10:00) 5. Vendor Invoice Received (11:30) 6. Match Invoice to PO (Feb 5) 7. Release Payment (Feb 6) 8. Close Case (Feb 7) This looks like the standard flow. Timing makes sense.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002: 1. Create PR (08:30) 2. Issue Purchase Order (08:40) - ANOMALY: PO issued BEFORE approval (approve happens later at 08:00 next day? No, wait let me check timestamps)

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait, looking at timestamps: - Create PR: 2024-02-01 08:30 - Issue Purchase Order: 2024-02-01 08:40 (10 mins later) - Vendor Invoice Received: 2024-02-03 07:00 - Release Payment: 2024-02-03 07:15 - Approve Purchase Requisition: 2024-02-03 08:00 - ANOMALY: Approval happens AFTER PO issuance. Also happens AFTER invoice received and payment released! This is completely out of order. PO issued without approval is a major control violation. Payment released before goods receipt! - Receive Goods: 2024-02-04 09:00 - happens after payment? Wait no, payment was Feb 3, goods received Feb 4. So payment before goods receipt! - Match Invoice to PO: 2024-02-05 - payment released BEFORE matching invoice to PO.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003: - Create PR (09:00) - Approve PR (09:10) - Issue PO (10:00) - Release Payment (Feb 8 11:00) - Vendor Invoice Received (Feb 9 12:00) - Receive Goods (Feb 10 13:00) - Match Invoice to PO (Feb 11) - Close Case (Feb 12) Anomaly: Release Payment happens BEFORE Vendor Invoice Received, BEFORE Receive Goods, and BEFORE Match Invoice to PO.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004: - Create PR (10:00) - Approve PR (10:15) - Receive Goods (Feb 6 09:00) - Issue Purchase Order (Feb 6 09:15) - Vendor Invoice Received (Feb 7) - Match Invoice to PO - Release Payment - Close Case Anomaly: Receive Goods happens BEFORE Issue Purchase Order.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Let me double check if there are other anomalies. Case 1004: Are there any timing issues with payment?

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Wait, actually could Receive Goods happen before Issue Purchase Order if its a rush shipment or standing order? In standard P2P, no. The PO authorizes the purchase; receiving goods before PO creates liability and control issues. Could Release Payment on Feb 3 at 07:15 be a prepayment? In some business contexts prepayments happen. However, standard P2P best practices usually require special workflows for prepayments, but in a generic P2P log without context, paying before goods receipt and before matching is anomalous.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So summary of anomalies: Case 1002: 1. Issue PO before Approve PR 2. Release Payment before Match Invoice to PO 3. Release Payment before Receive Goods 4. Release Payment before Approve PR Case 1003: 1. Release Payment before Receive Goods 2. Release Payment before Vendor Invoice Received 3. Release Payment before Match Invoice to PO Case 1004: 1. Receive Goods before Issue Purchase Order Standard P2P expected sequence: Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Vendor Invoice Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Comparisons: - 1001: Follows sequence perfectly. - 1002: Skips approval until far too late; pays before matching/receiving/approving. - 1003: Pays before anything after PO (goods, invoice, matching). - 1004: Receives goods before PO is issued.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should present these clearly, maybe using a table or structured format. For each case, identify the specific sequence deviations and explain the business risk (e.g., fraud, unauthorized spend, paying for undelivered goods, lack of audit trail, maverick buying). Let me structure the answer: 1. Overview of standard P2P flow for context. 2. Case-by-case analysis highlighting anomalies. 3. Summary table of violations. 4. Explanation of why problematic (control failures, financial risk, fraud, compliance).
